creator: amdmi3@amdmi3.ru
text:
* Aldis Berjoza (aldis@bsdroot.lv) wrote:

> I've fixed some of issues that you pointed out.
> 
> At first I didn't use cmake install because I didn't like that example
> binaries are placed into /usr/local/bin/. Now cmake installs
> everything except examples.... Examples, help and data files are
> installed in post-install.
> Q: Why is install help and data manually?
> A: Because cmake won't do it.
>
> About cmake.... for some reason it didn't work for me last time, but
> since then I reinstalled my desktop, and now it's OK.

Sorry, I haven't time to check this earlier. After the weekend if you
don't manage to fix problems mentioned below I'll help do it myself.
Well, there are still problems.

- You still should use PORTDOCS=* and PORTEXAMPLES=*. That means
  literally

PORTDOCS=*
PORTEXAMPLES=*

in the Makefile - with these, all files under DOCSDIR/EXAMPLES dir are
added to the plist automatically, so you can remove all 
%%PORTEXAMPLES%%%%EXAMPLESDIR%%... and PORTDOCS lines from the plist.

- I've misleaded you a bit with NOPORTDOCS - it needs doxygen, which is
  pretty weighty dependency for a library to be enabled by default, so
  we use DOXYGEN option and don't run doxygen if it's disabled. See
  audio/libfishsound as an example

- I don't really see a point for SRC/DATA vars, you can just set
  DISTFILES=Visualization_Library_SDK-${PORTVERSION}.tar.gz and then
  += it with data file if needed.

- Buch of patches for paths in examples - much better to use single
.for path in 3rdparty font glsl images models text volume ztest 
	@{REINPLACE_CMD} -e 's|"/${path}|"${path}|g' ${WRKSRC}/src/examples/Applets/App_*.hpp
.endfor

or you can replace it to ${PORTEXAMPLES} - relative path

- CMake stuff. Instead of installing files on your own, you should try
  to patch CMakeLists.txt. It seems that examples installation should be
  easily fixed with something like that

${REINPLACE_CMD} -Ee 's,^(.*(RUNTIME|LIBRARY|ARCHIVE) DESTINATION).*,\1${EXAMPLESDIR_REL},' ${WRKSRC}/src/examples/CMakeLists.txt

- In CMakeLists.txt, you should not use /usr/local, as ${LOCALBASE} may
  be different. You can REINPLACE_CMD /usr/local to ${LOCALBASE} in
  post-patch. In root CMakeLists.txt you don't need to directly specify 
  *_LIBRARY_DIR and *_INCLUDE_DIR, as corresponding FIND_PACKAGE do
  everything for you. The only problem is with WX, but you can leave
  that to me. Also I see no reason to comment out all options's, as
  they're overridden with CMAKE_ARGS anyway. 

-- 
Dmitry Marakasov   .   55B5 0596 FF1E 8D84 5F56  9510 D35A 80DD F9D2 F77D
